name: Island Biogeography Psychology Paradigm
mechanism: Island Biogeography (MacArthur & Wilson) in ecology operates through the interaction of species on an island as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through island area and distance from mainland, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—immigration and extinction rates reach equilibrium species number—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by species richness at equilibrium. This process is bounded by island size, isolation, colonization ability of species, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to psychology, the mechanism from ecology suggests that the core units of psychology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (island area and distance from mainland) maps onto the generative processes in psychology, while the selection pressure (immigration and extinction rates reach equilibrium species number) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that psychology systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in ecology, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (island size, isolation, colonization ability of species) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in psychology designed using ecology structural principles will show 28% increase in output quality compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=80 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Cascade Pattern Siteswap Stability Astrobiology Grammar
mechanism: Cascade Pattern Siteswap Stability in circus arts operates through the interaction of single throw-catch arc in a juggling pattern as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through throw height, dwell time, and hand-crossing angle differ across siteswap sequences, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—patterns where timing errors self-correct on subsequent throws persist in performance repertoire—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by mean time before pattern collapse (drop) under stochastic throw perturbation. This process is bounded by human reaction time floor, maximum trackable object count (~7), gravity-imposed flight-time minimum, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to astrobiology, the mechanism from circus arts suggests that the core units of astrobiology undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (throw height, dwell time, and hand-crossing angle differ across siteswap sequences) maps onto the generative processes in astrobiology, while the selection pressure (patterns where timing errors self-correct on subsequent throws persist in performance repertoire) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that astrobiology systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in circus arts, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (human reaction time floor, maximum trackable object count (~7), gravity-imposed flight-time minimum) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in astrobiology designed using circus arts structural principles will show 12% reduction in resource waste compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=60 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Hegelian Dialectic Parasitology Logic

name: Two-Process Model of Sleep Regulation Neuroscience Paradigm
mechanism: Two-Process Model of Sleep Regulation (Borbely) in sleep science operates through the interaction of sleep pressure / circadian phase as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through homeostatic sleep pressure (Process S) and circadian drive (Process C), creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—sleep onset when S exceeds upper threshold; wake when it falls below lower—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by sleep timing and duration optimized. This process is bounded by circadian period ~24h, ultradian oscillation of REM/NREM within sleep, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to neuroscience, the mechanism from sleep science suggests that the core units of neuroscience undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (homeostatic sleep pressure (Process S) and circadian drive (Process C)) maps onto the generative processes in neuroscience, while the selection pressure (sleep onset when S exceeds upper threshold; wake when it falls below lower) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that neuroscience systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in sleep science, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (circadian period ~24h, ultradian oscillation of REM/NREM within sleep) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in neuroscience designed using sleep science structural principles will show 40% faster convergence rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=80 cases over a 18-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Rottemae Tempo-Silence Ratio Machine Learning Algebra
mechanism: Rottemae Tempo-Silence Ratio in tea ceremony operates through the interaction of interval of deliberate silence (ma) between procedural movements as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through host temperament, ceremony style (wabi vs formal), and guest count modulate the pace of pauses, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—silence durations that allow guests to absorb each action without creating restlessness are reinforced through training—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by collective meditative state (ichigo ichie) achieved by the gathering. This process is bounded by guest attention span, ceremony total time budget, seasonal time-of-day light constraints, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.

This cross-domain mapping predicts that machine learning systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in tea ceremony, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (guest attention span, ceremony total time budget, seasonal time-of-day light constraints) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in machine learning designed using tea ceremony structural principles will show 40% faster convergence rates compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=200 cases over a 8-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Plateau's Law Triple Cybernetics System
mechanism: Plateau's Law Triple Junction Equilibrium in soap bubble physics operates through the interaction of triple junction (Plateau border) where three bubble films meet as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through film curvatures, surface tension anisotropy, and bubble size ratios alter junction geometry, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—junctions that satisfy the 120-degree dihedral angle rule are mechanically stable; others rearrange instantly—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by persistence time of the junction configuration before topological rearrangement (T1 event). This process is bounded by foam drainage reducing film thickness below critical rupture threshold (~100 nm), four-film vertices are always unstable, establishing the conditions under which the mechanism produces reliable outcomes. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
application: When transplanted to cybernetics, the mechanism from soap bubble physics suggests that the core units of cybernetics undergo an analogous evolutionary process. The variation mechanism (film curvatures, surface tension anisotropy, and bubble size ratios alter junction geometry) maps onto the generative processes in cybernetics, while the selection pressure (junctions that satisfy the 120-degree dihedral angle rule are mechanically stable; others rearrange instantly) corresponds to the evaluative forces that determine which innovations, strategies, or structures persist in the target domain. This cross-domain mapping predicts that cybernetics systems will exhibit phase transitions, path dependencies, and fitness landscape topologies that mirror those observed in soap bubble physics, but expressed through domain-specific substrates and timescales. The boundary conditions (foam drainage reducing film thickness below critical rupture threshold (~100 nm), four-film vertices are always unstable) constrain where the analogy holds and where it breaks down.
prediction: Systems in cybernetics designed using soap bubble physics structural principles will show 18% greater diversity indices compared to conventional approaches, measurable across a sample of n=40 cases over a 24-month observation period using domain-standard assessment instruments.

name: Locard Trace Transfer Persistence Dance Paradigm
mechanism: Locard Trace Transfer Persistence in forensic science operates through the interaction of trace evidence particle transferred at contact as the fundamental unit of analysis. Variation arises through contact pressure, surface texture, and material adhesion produce different transfer quantities and retention rates, creating a diverse population of candidates that face differential pressures. The selection mechanism—only particles with sufficient adhesion to substrate survive environmental degradation, handling, and collection procedures—determines which variants persist and propagate, while fitness is measured by evidential value measured by traceability to source with statistical exclusion of alternative origins. The structural insight is that any domain exhibiting analogous unit-variation-selection dynamics should show comparable emergent patterns regardless of substrate.
